summary: Verified checkout price mismatch bug fix. Bug was: Checkout showed ₹103 but Razorpay asked for ₹197. Root cause: Weight calculation in backend was using raw item['weight'] field (containing grams as integer, e.g., 50 for 50g) but treating it as kg (resulting in 150kg for 3 items!). Fix: Added _calculate_shipping_weight_kg() helper function that correctly calculates 0.08kg per 50g pack and 0.35kg per 200g jar with 0.25kg minimum.

Bug Fix Verification - Weight Calculatio.test_create_order_with_rto_voucher_price: PASSED - Final total ₹103 matches Razorpay amount 10300 paise
Bug Fix Verification - Weight Calculatio.test_single_50g_pack_weight: PASSED - Single item weight calculation correct
Bug Fix Verification - Weight Calculatio.test_three_50g_packs_exact_scenario: PASSED - Exact bug scenario (3x50g + RTO voucher + PIN 812002) returns correct pricing

bug_fix_details.original_bug: Checkout showed ₹103 but Razorpay asked for ₹197
bug_fix_details.root_cause: Weight calculation in backend orders.py used item['weight'] field (50 for 50g pack) as kg instead of grams. 3 x 50g items calculated as 150kg instead of 0.24kg
bug_fix_details.fix_description: Added _calculate_shipping_weight_kg() function that calculates: 50g pack = 0.08kg (80g with packaging), 200g jar = 0.35kg (350g with packaging), minimum 0.25kg

critical_code_review_comments: ✓ _calculate_shipping_weight_kg() function correctly handles both 50g and 200g sizes
✓ Function is consistent with frontend calculatePackageWeight() in CheckoutPage.jsx
✓ ShipRocket rate fetched with correct weight, capped at slab maximum

success_rate.frontend: UI loads correctly, checkout flow works

seed_data_creation: None - used existing RTO voucher RTO-EF7DE05D
context_for_next_testing_agent: Weight calculation bug is fixed. The _calculate_shipping_weight_kg() function in backend/routers/orders.py correctly calculates shipping weight. Test file /app/backend/tests/test_checkout_weight_fix.py contains comprehensive tests for this fix including the exact bug scenario (3x50g + RTO voucher + PIN 812002).

rca_of_the_issue.symptom: User sees ₹103 on checkout page but Razorpay asks for ₹197
rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: Backend orders.py was using item['weight'] field directly for ShipRocket rate calculation. The weight field contains grams as integer (50 for 50g pack). Code was treating this as kg, so 3 x 50g = 150kg instead of 0.24kg. ShipRocket returned very high rates for 150kg, which got capped at the maximum slab rate (₹149 instead of ~₹55)
rca_of_the_issue.fix_applied: Added _calculate_shipping_weight_kg() helper function that maps product sizes to correct weights: 50g = 0.08kg, 200g = 0.35kg, minimum 0.25kg
rca_of_the_issue.verification_method: API test with exact bug scenario confirms final_total matches razorpay.amount
